liveSmokeChecks: GET /api/orgs/default/snapshot returns structured statuses for kubernetes, gitea, agentMux, controller, assistant, and externalProviders without leaking secrets.
GET /api/orgs/default/agents/events/stream returns text/event-stream, emits a connected event, supports heartbeat, and closes cleanly.
Two process instances using the same NATS/JetStream configuration receive the same emitted resource-change event.
An SSE subscriber reconnecting with Last-Event-ID or the configured cursor receives missed durable events.
When NATS is unavailable in production mode, health/reporting surfaces show degraded or error state instead of silently claiming durable delivery.

health: Gitea probes KRATE_GITEA_HTTP_URL/api/v1/version with timeout and structured failure.
Agent Mux probes AGENT_MUX_URL/healthz or AGENT_GATEWAY_URL/healthz with timeout and structured failure.
Krate Controller probes KRATE_CONTROLLER_URL/healthz with timeout and structured failure.
Kubernetes connectivity is represented explicitly using bounded kubectl cluster-info or an injectable equivalent.
Assistant credential validation defaults to presence/format checks and never leaks key material.
Probe results are concurrent, partial, cache-control no-store, and compatible with HealthMonitor status rendering.
events: Production event transport is broker-backed with NATS JetStream or an explicitly justified equivalent.
Event persistence is durable and queryable; production replay does not depend only on the old local JSONL ring buffer.
Core HTTP SSE and web SSE routes subscribe through the shared abstraction and support replay cursors.
Cross-replica fanout works when multiple server instances share broker configuration.
Slow subscribers and closed streams do not block emitters.
Broker-unavailable behavior is explicit in health/reporting and tests.
delivery: No unrelated dirty worktree files are committed.
Helm values/templates wire NATS and probe env vars without committing secrets.
Docs are updated where behavior or env vars changed.
PR links #612 and summarizes verification evidence.
